mnemonics: PROPOSE a Move: In Metropolis-Hastings, the Proposal distribution suggests (PROPOSES) the next candidate state for the Markov Chain.
keyPoints: The proposal distribution, often denoted as q(x'|x), suggests a new candidate sample (x') given the current state (x).
It is a critical component of the Metropolis-Hastings algorithm, a type of Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) method.
The choice of proposal distribution greatly affects the efficiency and convergence rate of the sampling process.
commonMistakes: Choosing a proposal distribution with too narrow a variance, leading to slow exploration and high autocorrelation between samples.
Choosing a proposal distribution with too wide a variance, resulting in a low acceptance rate and inefficient sampling.
